meta.purpose: Simulate realistic claim scenarios across all 9 B-1/B-2 plans. Scores are probabilistic — not legal determinations. Always based on evidence.json records. Confidence reflects quality of underlying document.
meta.disclaimer: These are research estimates only. Actual claim outcomes depend on specific circumstances, attending physician documentation, carrier adjudication, and plan terms in effect at time of claim. Never represent these as coverage guarantees.

scoring.coverage_score: 0-100. How well the plan covers this scenario. 90-100 = very well covered; 60-89 = covered with significant limitations; 30-59 = partially covered with major gaps; 0-29 = likely denied or near-worthless coverage.
scoring.denial_risk: 0-100. Probability of claim denial or significant underpayment. 0-20 = low risk; 21-50 = moderate risk; 51-80 = high risk; 81-100 = very likely denied.
scoring.confidence: 0-100. Confidence in the simulation based on document quality. 90-100 = Certificate L1 with explicit language; 60-89 = Certificate with inferred application; 40-59 = Policy Wording L2 or partial docs; 0-39 = brochure/marketing only.

scenarios.name: ER Visit — New Illness (Appendicitis, No Pre-existing History)
scenarios.description: 55-year-old Indian visitor on B-1/B-2. No pre-existing conditions. Develops sudden abdominal pain at 10pm. Goes to ER. Diagnosed with appendicitis. ER visit + CT scan + labs. Total billed: $8,500. No admission yet.

Patriot_America_Plus.estimated_plan_payout: $7,350–$8,000 (after $250 deductible + $250 ER illness copay; 90% OON or 100% in-network)
key_risks: ER illness extra $250 deductible applies
Out-of-network penalty 10% if not PPO provider
Pre-certification recommended for CT scan
Patriot_America_Plus.notes: New illness, not pre-existing. Appendicitis is clearly an acute new condition. Main risk is OON penalty and ER illness deductible.

key_risks: ER illness $250 extra deductible applies
Pre-certification recommended
Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: 100% coinsurance everywhere. Only risk is ER illness copay and pre-certification for imaging.

CoverAmerica_Gold.estimated_plan_payout: ~$6,200–$7,800 (after deductible; 100% in-network / 80% OON with $1K OOP max)
key_risks: No $0 deductible option — minimum $100
80% OON coinsurance (weaker than IMG)
COVID-19 waiting period not applicable here
CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: Appendicitis is covered. Slightly weaker OON coinsurance (80% vs 90%).

Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.estimated_plan_payout: $7,800–$8,200 (100% coinsurance everywhere; after deductible; $200 ER illness copay)
key_risks: $200 ER illness extra deductible (slightly less than IMG's $250)
No PPO network restriction — 100% everywhere
Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.notes: 100% coinsurance is a strength. No PPO penalty. ER illness $200 copay applies.

VisitorSecure.estimated_plan_payout: $375–$785 (ER fixed benefit IF hospitalized; $0 if not admitted)
key_risks: SCHEDULED BENEFIT — ER illness benefit only paid if admitted to hospital
Appendicitis diagnosis in ER (pre-surgical) may not trigger admission benefit
CT scan and labs may not be separately reimbursed at actual cost
Fixed daily caps bear no relation to actual $8,500 billed
VisitorSecure.notes: CRITICAL — VisitorSecure ER illness benefit requires hospital admission. If not admitted from ER, payout could be $0. Even with admission, fixed caps leave large gap on $8,500 bill.

Atlas_America.estimated_plan_payout: ~$8,000 (100% coinsurance; $200 ER copay; UHC PPO network)
key_risks: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — full Certificate not obtained
$200 ER co-payment per visit
Atlas_America.notes: Based on brochure/confirmation data. UHC PPO network is strong for USA. Full cert needed to confirm.

INF_Elite_X.estimated_plan_payout: $5,500–$7,000 (after deductible; 80-90% in-network; hospital ancillary capped at $20K)
key_risks: In-network coinsurance 80-90% (insured pays 10-20% even in-network)
Hospital ancillary services (CT scan, ER use) capped at $20,000 total
Out-of-network exposure 30-40% if not in-network
5-day illness waiting period if purchased mid-trip
INF_Elite_X.notes: Appendicitis is a new illness — not pre-existing. Covered at 80-90% in-network. Hospital ancillary $20K cap is adequate for this scenario. Main risk: in-network coinsurance not 100%.

SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.estimated_plan_payout: $7,900–$8,300 (after deductible; 100% in-network; $100 ER copay; 80% OON first $10K)
key_risks: $100 ER copay per visit
80% OON coinsurance on first $10K (OOP max $2K)
SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: 100% in-network. $100 ER copay is lower risk. Appendicitis clearly new illness.

SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.estimated_plan_payout: $8,000–$8,350 (after deductible; 100% in-network; $100 ER copay; 90% OON first $10K)
key_risks: $100 ER copay per visit
90% OON coinsurance on first $10K (OOP max $1K)
SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: Better OON coinsurance than Basic. Same ER copay.

scenarios.name: ER Visit + Hospitalization — Injury (Fall, Hip Fracture)
scenarios.description: 68-year-old Indian visitor. Slips and falls, fractures hip. ER visit + X-ray + surgery + 4-day hospital stay + ICU overnight. Total billed: $65,000.

Patriot_America_Plus.estimated_plan_payout: $62,000–$64,500 (injury ER 100% in-network; surgery + ICU 100%; after deductible)
key_risks: No ER injury extra deductible (only illness has extra $250)
Pre-certification required for surgery and hospitalization
Must use in-network providers for 100%
Patriot_America_Plus.notes: Injury (not illness) — no ER extra deductible. 100% in-network. Strong coverage for this scenario.

key_risks: Pre-certification required
Documentation requirements
Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: Best coverage for this scenario. 100% everywhere, no OON penalty, no ER injury copay.

key_risks: 80% OON coinsurance — OOP max $1K limits exposure
No ER injury extra deductible
CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: OOP max of $1K on OON expenses limits patient exposure for large claim.

Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.estimated_plan_payout: $62,000–$64,500 (100% everywhere; no PPO penalty; after deductible)
key_risks: No PPO network restriction is a strength for injury (any hospital)
Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.notes: 100% coinsurance everywhere means no penalty if taken to nearest hospital (not PPO). Strong for emergency trauma.

key_risks: SCHEDULED BENEFIT — plan pays fixed amounts regardless of actual $65,000 bill
ICU 1 day = $2,200
Surgery $3,600–$7,800 per session
TOTAL ESTIMATED PAYOUT: ~$12,000–$20,000 on a $65,000 bill
VisitorSecure.notes: CRITICAL — scheduled benefit plan leaves $45K+ out-of-pocket on a $65K injury. This plan is NOT suitable for serious injuries or surgeries.

key_risks: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — full cert not obtained
Atlas_America.notes: Based on brochure. 100% coinsurance with UHC PPO should cover well.

INF_Elite_X.estimated_plan_payout: $45,000–$55,000 (80-90% in-network; BUT hospital room capped $20K + ancillary $20K = $40K combined hospital sub-limit)
key_risks: Hospital room + board: capped at $20,000 regardless of actual cost
Hospital ancillary (surgery, ER, OR): capped at $20,000 separately
Combined hospital sub-limit: $40,000 max on actual hospital charges
Surgery likely fits within $20K ancillary cap for single fracture
ICU room included in hospital room $20K cap
INF_Elite_X.notes: Sub-limits create exposure on $65K bill. Hospital room $20K + ancillary $20K = $40K maximum from hospital charges. Plus doctor fees, imaging at coinsurance rate. Patient may owe $15K–$25K.

key_risks: $100 ER copay
OON exposure limited to $2,000 max
SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: No sub-limits for injury scenario. URC up to Medical Maximum. Strong for injury.

key_risks: $100 ER copay
OON exposure limited to $1,000 max
SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: Better OON limit than Basic. No sub-limits for injury.

scenarios.name: Acute Cardiac Event — Known Pre-existing Heart Disease
scenarios.description: 64-year-old Indian visitor with known coronary artery disease (pre-existing). Sudden severe chest pain, rushed to ER. Heart attack confirmed. Emergency angioplasty + 3-day ICU + 2-day regular stay. Total billed: $120,000.
scenarios.category: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Condition — Cardiac

Patriot_America_Plus.verdict: LIKELY COVERED — up to plan maximum via Acute Onset
Patriot_America_Plus.estimated_plan_payout: Up to plan maximum (e.g., $100K if $100K plan; $1M if $1M plan) after deductible
key_risks: Must meet ALL acute onset criteria: sudden/unexpected, within 24hr treatment, no Rx change in 30 days, not traveling for treatment, stable 30 days prior
Coronary artery disease is a known chronic condition — carrier will scrutinize 'no Rx change' and '30-day stability' criteria
If insured recently changed cardiac medications → claim DENIED
3-year lookback pre-existing definition — CAD almost certainly pre-existing
Pre-certification required — call immediately on the way to hospital
Patriot_America_Plus.notes: Acute onset coverage is the insured's only path. Coverage is up to plan max which is favorable. But denial risk is elevated because cardiac chronic conditions face high scrutiny on stability criteria.

Patriot_America_Platinum.verdict: LIKELY COVERED — Non-US citizens up to $1M via Acute Onset
Patriot_America_Platinum.estimated_plan_payout: Up to $1,000,000 (non-US citizen, age ≤69) after deductible
key_risks: Same acute onset criteria as Plus
Same 30-day stability/no Rx change requirement
Best payout ceiling of all plans if criteria met
Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: Best acute onset limit for non-US citizens. $1M covers this scenario fully. Same denial risks as Plus on stability criteria.

key_risks: CARDIAC SUB-LIMIT: $36,000 for angina, heart attacks, strokes (age ≤69)
$120,000 billed → patient owes ~$84,000 after $36K plan payment + deductible
Same acute onset criteria apply
This is the MOST RESTRICTIVE acute onset cardiac coverage among all plans
CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: CRITICAL WEAKNESS — $36K cardiac sub-limit is far below $120K billed. This plan performs poorly for cardiac pre-existing conditions.

key_risks: CARDIAC SUB-LIMIT: $25,000 for cardiac conditions and stroke
$120,000 billed → patient owes ~$95,000
30-day stability requirement applies
Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.notes: Cardiac sub-limit is even lower than CoverAmerica Gold ($25K vs $36K). Not suitable for cardiac pre-existing conditions at this cost level.

VisitorSecure.estimated_plan_payout: $5,000–$10,000 estimated (scheduled benefits for ICU + room + surgery; pre-existing exclusion likely applies)
key_risks: SCHEDULED BENEFIT — fixed caps regardless of $120K bill
Pre-existing cardiac condition likely excluded unless acute onset criteria met
Even if acute onset applies, fixed scheduled amounts cap payout far below actual cost
VisitorSecure.notes: WORST OUTCOME — double risk: scheduled benefit caps AND pre-existing exclusion. Potentially $110K+ out of pocket.

Atlas_America.estimated_plan_payout: Unknown — acute onset cardiac terms not confirmed
key_risks: Full Certificate not obtained — acute onset cardiac sub-limits unknown
Atlas_America.notes: Cannot simulate accurately without full Certificate. NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW.

INF_Elite_X.verdict: PARTIALLY COVERED — pre-existing condition covered with sub-limits
INF_Elite_X.estimated_plan_payout: $25,000–$50,000 (pre-ex max $25K–$50K for age 0–69; sub-limits per service type)
key_risks: Pre-existing covered but capped: max $25K–$50K total
Per-service sub-limits within pre-ex max: Hospital/ICU room $15K, Surgery $15K, Hospital Misc $15K, ER $7,500
DOES NOT use acute onset model — pre-ex is actually covered
VERIFY: coronary artery disease managed on stable medication — if no change in 24 months, may NOT be pre-existing under INF definition
If not pre-existing (stable meds 24 months): covered at full plan maximum up to $1M
INF_Elite_X.notes: UNIQUE ADVANTAGE: If cardiac meds stable for 24+ months with no changes, INF's stable-medication exception means CAD is NOT pre-existing → covered at full $1M plan max. If meds changed recently → pre-ex sub-limits apply ($25K–$50K). Best plan if stable medication criteria met.

SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.estimated_plan_payout: $24,000–$25,000 maximum (acute onset limit $25K total; no cardiac sub-limit separation)
key_risks: Acute onset limit: $25,000 total for age ≤69
$120K billed → patient owes ~$95,000
Acute onset definition EXCLUDES 'previously diagnosed chronic condition with expected episodes or flare-ups' — CRITICAL: CAD with known episodes may fail criterion (f)
7-day waiting period must have passed
HIGH denial risk on criterion (f) — chronic cardiac condition
SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: Very limited — $25K cap. Worse than Plus/Platinum by far. High denial risk because chronic CAD with expected episodes is explicitly excluded from acute onset definition.

key_risks: Cardiac sub-limit: $50,000 (better than Basic's $25K but still far short of $120K)
Patient owes ~$70,000
Same chronic condition exclusion risk in acute onset definition
7-day waiting period
SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: Better than Basic for cardiac but still limited. $50K cardiac acute onset still leaves $70K patient exposure on this scenario.

scenarios.name: Emergency Medical Evacuation — Remote Location
scenarios.description: 70-year-old Indian visitor has medical emergency while traveling in a rural area. Needs air ambulance evacuation to nearest major hospital. Evacuation cost: $85,000.

key_risks: Must be pre-approved and coordinated by Company
Age 70 = medical maximum reduced but evacuation may be separate limit
Patriot_America_Plus.notes: $85K well within $1M evacuation limit. Must call Company first.

Patriot_America_Platinum.verdict: LIKELY COVERED — up to full plan maximum
Patriot_America_Platinum.estimated_plan_payout: $85,000 (evacuation = plan maximum; $85K well within any tier)
key_risks: Must be coordinated by Company
Age 70: medical max drops to $100K but evacuation limit = full plan max
Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: Best evacuation of all plans. $85K trivially covered.

CoverAmerica_Gold.estimated_plan_payout: $85,000 (evacuation limit $1M; pre-existing evacuation also covered — unique feature)
key_risks: Must be coordinated by Company
Age 70+ acute onset cardiac not covered — but if evacuation is for non-pre-existing, full $1M applies
CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: Evacuation includes pre-existing conditions — unique advantage.

key_risks: Acute onset pre-existing evacuation limited to $25,000 — if the evacuation is due to a pre-existing condition, only $25K applies
Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.notes: If cause is new illness: $1M applies. If cause is acute onset of pre-existing: only $25K. Cause determination is critical.

VisitorSecure.estimated_plan_payout: $0 — evacuation only available for members under age 70
key_risks: CRITICAL: Evacuation benefit explicitly not available for age 70+
VisitorSecure.notes: ZERO evacuation coverage at age 70. $85,000 entirely patient responsibility.

Atlas_America.estimated_plan_payout: Unknown — evacuation limit not confirmed for age 70+
key_risks: Full Certificate not obtained

INF_Elite_X.estimated_plan_payout: $25,000 maximum ($25K evacuation limit — $60,000 patient out of pocket on $85K bill)
key_risks: CRITICAL WEAKNESS: $25,000 evacuation limit — lowest of all plans
$85K evacuation → patient owes $60,000 after $25K plan payment
Age 70+ visitors face highest evacuation risk; INF provides worst protection
INF_Elite_X.notes: INF Elite X has the worst evacuation coverage ($25K). Air ambulances routinely cost $50K–$200K. Evacuation risk is unmitigated.

key_risks: Must be arranged by Seven Corners Assist
Separate from Medical Maximum
SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: $250K evacuation covers this $85K scenario. Must pre-arrange through Seven Corners Assist.

key_risks: Must be arranged by Seven Corners Assist
SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: $500K evacuation is excellent coverage for this scenario.

scenarios.name: Physical Therapy — 8 Visits After Minor Injury
scenarios.description: 45-year-old visitor. Minor back injury. Doctor prescribes 8 visits of outpatient physical therapy. PT billed at $150/visit × 8 = $1,200 total.

key_risks: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — no visit cap confirmed from Certificate; if visit cap exists, payout may be lower
Patriot_America_Plus.notes: PT covered per evidence.json but no explicit visit cap found. Full Certificate review needed.

key_risks: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — no visit cap confirmed
Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: Same as Plus for this scenario. 100% everywhere if covered.

key_risks: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — no visit cap confirmed
Requires medical order/treatment plan
CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: PT covered with medical order. Visit cap unknown.

Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.estimated_plan_payout: $500 maximum (PT capped at $50/visit × 10 visits; 8 visits = $400 at $50/visit)
key_risks: $50/visit cap vs $150 billed — plan pays $50, patient pays $100 per visit regardless of coinsurance
10-visit maximum per period
Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.notes: Plan pays $50/visit regardless of actual $150 billed. Patient owes $100/visit = $800 out-of-pocket on 8 visits. Very limited PT coverage.

key_risks: $40/visit fixed benefit vs $150 billed — patient pays $110/visit
12-visit maximum
VisitorSecure.notes: SCHEDULED BENEFIT — $40 per visit is fixed regardless of actual cost. Patient owes $880 on 8 visits at $150 each.

key_risks: $50/visit cap vs $150 billed — plan pays $50, patient pays $100/visit
$500 total cap (10 visits)
INF_Elite_X.notes: Same PT cap as Safe Travels. Very limited.

key_risks: EXCLUDED — 'You are not covered for Physiotherapy or Chiropractic Care'
SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: ZERO PT coverage. Patient owes full $1,200.

key_risks: $50/visit vs $150 billed — plan pays $50, patient pays $100/visit
SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: PT covered unlike Basic. Same $500 cap limit.

scenarios.name: COVID-19 Treatment — Hospitalization
scenarios.description: 58-year-old visitor. Contracts COVID-19 and develops severe symptoms. Hospitalized 5 days. Total billed: $25,000. Plan was purchased for 10-day trip.

Patriot_America_Plus.notes: COVID covered as any illness. No waiting period restriction.

Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: Same as Plus. No COVID restriction.

CoverAmerica_Gold.verdict: NOT COVERED — 30-day purchase requirement not met
CoverAmerica_Gold.estimated_plan_payout: $0 — plan purchased for 10-day trip; COVID requires 30 days initial coverage
key_risks: CRITICAL: COVID-19 explicitly requires plan purchased for at least 30 days
10-day trip → NO COVID-19 coverage at all
CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: CRITICAL FAILURE for short-trip buyers. $25,000 entirely patient responsibility.

VisitorSecure.estimated_plan_payout: Unknown — COVID coverage not confirmed from available documents
VisitorSecure.notes: Even if covered, scheduled benefit caps severely limit COVID hospitalization payout.

Atlas_America.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — based on brochure data.

INF_Elite_X.estimated_plan_payout: Unknown — COVID not explicitly addressed in Policy Wording
INF_Elite_X.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW. COVID not mentioned in Policy Wording. If covered as illness: 80-90% in-network after deductible. Hospital ancillary cap $20K could limit hospitalization coverage.

SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: COVID covered same as any illness. No waiting period.

SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: Same as Basic. No COVID restriction.

scenarios.name: New Cancer Diagnosis During Trip
scenarios.description: 52-year-old visitor. No known cancer history. Develops unexplained weight loss + cough. ER visit leads to imaging → new lung cancer diagnosis. Biopsy + oncologist consult. Total billed: $18,000 for diagnostic workup.

Patriot_America_Plus.verdict: LIKELY COVERED — new diagnosis, not pre-existing
key_risks: Carrier may argue cancer existed (undiagnosed) within 3-year lookback — potential pre-existing dispute
NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — no explicit cancer exclusion found but 3-year pre-ex definition could apply if symptomatic
Patriot_America_Plus.notes: New first-time diagnosis should be covered but carrier may dispute if symptoms existed before Effective Date.

key_risks: Same pre-existing dispute risk as Plus
Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: 100% coinsurance. Same risk of pre-existing dispute.

CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: No specific cancer exclusion found. Same pre-existing dispute risk.

Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.notes: No cancer exclusion found in evidence. Pre-existing dispute risk.

VisitorSecure.verdict: PARTIALLY COVERED — scheduled benefit caps apply
VisitorSecure.estimated_plan_payout: $3,000–$6,000 (fixed caps for imaging, labs, office visits)
VisitorSecure.notes: Scheduled benefit caps severely limit diagnostic workup payout.

key_risks: CRITICAL: 'expenses incurred for any condition directly or indirectly related to or caused by cancer' EXCLUDED
Even new cancer diagnosis is excluded under this broad language
Patient owes full $18,000
INF_Elite_X.notes: COMPLETE EXCLUSION. INF Elite X is unsuitable for any traveler with cancer risk.

SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.verdict: LIKELY COVERED — no cancer exclusion found
key_risks: Pre-existing dispute risk if symptoms pre-date coverage
SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: No cancer exclusion in Certificate. New diagnosis should be covered.

SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.verdict: LIKELY COVERED — no cancer exclusion found
SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: Same as Basic. No cancer exclusion.

scenarios.name: Stable Diabetic Visitor — Diabetic Ketoacidosis Emergency
scenarios.description: 60-year-old visitor with Type 2 diabetes (known pre-existing), managed on fixed daily metformin for 3 years with no medication changes. Develops diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) during trip — a sudden acute episode. ER + 2-day ICU. Total billed: $45,000.
scenarios.category: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Condition — Diabetes

Patriot_America_Plus.verdict: LIKELY COVERED via Acute Onset — moderate denial risk
Patriot_America_Plus.estimated_plan_payout: Up to plan maximum (e.g., $45K on $100K plan) after deductible
key_risks: Must meet all acute onset criteria: sudden, Medical Emergency, treated within 24hr, no Rx change in 30 days, stable 30 days prior
DKA is a sudden acute emergency — meets 'sudden/unexpected' criterion
Metformin unchanged for 3 years → 30-day stability criterion easily met
RISK: Carrier may argue DKA is a 'known chronic condition with expected episodes or flare-ups' if insured had prior DKA history
Patriot_America_Plus.notes: Good acute onset case IF first-ever DKA episode and medication stable. Main risk is prior DKA history. Recommend documenting stability at departure.

Patriot_America_Platinum.notes: Same as Plus but higher ceiling. $1M limit covers $45K easily.

CoverAmerica_Gold.estimated_plan_payout: Up to plan maximum (DKA is not cardiac — no cardiac sub-limit)
CoverAmerica_Gold.notes: DKA is not a cardiac condition so $36K cardiac sub-limit doesn't apply. Full plan max applies if acute onset criteria met.

Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.estimated_plan_payout: Up to Medical Maximum (DKA not cardiac; no cardiac sub-limit)
Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive.notes: 30-day stability applies. DKA meets sudden/unexpected criterion.

VisitorSecure.verdict: PARTIALLY COVERED — scheduled benefit + pre-existing risk
VisitorSecure.notes: Even if acute onset applies, scheduled benefit caps mean massive gap on $45K bill.

INF_Elite_X.verdict: LIKELY WELL COVERED — stable medication exception applies
INF_Elite_X.estimated_plan_payout: $40,000–$44,000 (if stable medication exception applies: NOT pre-existing → full plan maximum at 80-90% in-network)
key_risks: FAVORABLE: Metformin unchanged for 3 years → INF's 24-month stable medication exception applies → diabetes NOT pre-existing under INF definition
DKA treated at full plan coverage rate (80-90% in-network), not pre-ex sub-limits
RISK: If metformin dose was ever adjusted → exception fails → pre-ex sub-limits apply ($25K–$50K max)
INF_Elite_X.notes: BEST PLAN FOR THIS SCENARIO if stable medication condition is met. INF's unique definition turns 'stable diabetic' into 'not pre-existing.' $45K bill covered at 80-90% in-network after deductible.

SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.verdict: PARTIALLY COVERED — $25K acute onset limit + high denial risk
key_risks: Acute onset limit $25K — patient owes $20K on $45K bill
Criterion (f) risk: DKA in diabetic is arguably a 'chronic condition with expected episodes'
High denial risk if carrier argues DKA is expected in diabetic
SC_Travel_Medical_Basic.notes: Moderate denial risk on the 'expected episodes' criterion. $25K cap leaves $20K gap even if approved.

SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.verdict: PARTIALLY COVERED — $75K acute onset (non-cardiac), high denial risk
key_risks: Non-cardiac acute onset: $75K — covers $45K DKA if approved
Same criterion (f) denial risk: DKA as 'expected episode' in diabetic
7-day waiting period must have passed
SC_Travel_Medical_Choice.notes: If approved, Choice pays full $45K (within $75K non-cardiac limit). But same elevated denial risk as Basic on criterion (f).

scenario_summary.best_overall_coverage: Patriot_America_Platinum — 100% coinsurance everywhere, evacuation = plan max, $1M acute onset for non-US citizens
scenario_summary.best_for_preexisting_chronic: INF_Elite_X — unique stable medication exception; best if meds stable 24+ months. BUT weak evacuation ($25K) and cancer excluded.
scenario_summary.worst_for_serious_claims: VisitorSecure — scheduled benefit plan leaves $45K–$100K+ out-of-pocket on major injuries/illnesses
scenario_summary.covid_trap: CoverAmerica_Gold — zero COVID coverage on trips shorter than 30 days
scenario_summary.cardiac_trap: CoverAmerica_Gold ($36K) and Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive ($25K) have the lowest cardiac acute onset sub-limits
scenario_summary.evacuation_trap: INF_Elite_X ($25K) and VisitorSecure ($50K, age <70 only) have dangerously low evacuation limits
scenario_summary.cancer_trap: INF_Elite_X — complete cancer exclusion including new diagnosis
